sections.paragraph: m. an axe, with suff. קַרְדֻּמֹּו 1Sa 13:20 pl. קַרְדֻּמִּים 1Sa 13:21, and קַרְדֻּמֹּות Psa 74:5 Jer 46:22 also קַרְדֻּמֹות (without Dag.) Jud. 9:48 . (Arab. قَدُّومُ, Talmud קורדום id. I suppose the Hebrew קַרְדֹּם to be from the verbal Piel קַדֹּם, قَدُّومُ the letter ר being inserted (see ), from קָדַם in the primary signification of being sharp; compare גָּזַם, קָסַם. Another and softer form of this same word appears to be גַּרְזֶן, where compare the Arabic forms.)
